title: Lifetime Transfer
description: A gift or transfer made during the testator's lifetime, relevant for tax calculations such as the UK 7-year rule, US unified gift/estate tax, and similar lookback provisions in other jurisdictions.

id.description: Unique identifier for this lifetime transfer within the INHERIT document.

donorPersonId.description: Reference to the Person.id of the donor (typically the testator).

doneePersonIds.description: Person IDs of the recipients of the transfer.

transferType.$comment: gift: outright gift. potentially_exempt_transfer: UK PET (exempt if donor survives 7 years). chargeable_lifetime_transfer: UK CLT (into trust). gift_with_reservation: donor retains benefit. failed_pet: PET where donor died within 7 years. settlement: transfer into a settlement/trust. advancement: advance of inheritance. loan_forgiveness: debt written off. sale_at_undervalue: deliberate deprivation. gift_of_shares: transfer of company shares. other: any other type.

value.description: Value of the transfer at the date it was made, in minor currency units.

assetId.description: Reference to the Asset.id of the asset transferred, if it is still recorded in the document.

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property.id of the property transferred.

valuationId.description: Reference to a Valuation entity providing a formal valuation of the transferred asset.

type.$comment: annual_exemption: UK annual exemption (currently GBP 3,000 per tax year). small_gift: UK small gifts exemption (GBP 250 per recipient per tax year). marriage_gift: UK exemption for gifts in consideration of marriage or civil partnership. normal_expenditure: UK exemption for gifts from surplus income as part of normal expenditure. business_property_relief: UK BPR — relief on transfers of qualifying business assets. agricultural_property_relief: UK APR — relief on transfers of qualifying agricultural property. spouse_exemption: transfers between spouses or civil partners (exempt in most jurisdictions). charity_exemption: transfers to qualifying charities. taper_relief: UK taper relief reducing IHT on failed PETs between 3 and 7 years. annual_exclusion: US annual gift tax exclusion (per donee per year). lifetime_exemption: US unified lifetime gift/estate tax exemption. gift_splitting: US election to split gifts between spouses. other: any other exemption or relief not covered above.
type.description: The type of exemption or relief claimed against this transfer.

amount.description: Amount of the exemption claimed, in minor currency units.

notes.description: Free-text notes about this exemption claim.
examples: Used remaining GBP 1,500 of annual exemption carried forward from previous tax year
Gift splitting election filed jointly with spouse on Form 709

exemptionsClaimed.description: Exemptions and reliefs claimed against this transfer to reduce its taxable value.

taxReturnFiled.description: Whether a gift tax return or similar filing has been submitted for this transfer.

taxPaid.description: Amount of tax paid on this transfer, in minor currency units.

lookbackRelevant.description: Whether this transfer falls within the jurisdiction's lookback period for tax purposes (e.g. 7 years in the UK, 3 years in the US for certain gifts).

lookbackYearsRemaining.description: Years remaining in the lookback period. In the UK, this counts down from 7 — a PET made 4 years before death has 3 years remaining.

taperReliefPercentage.description: UK taper relief percentage reducing the IHT charge on failed PETs and CLTs. Ranges from 0% (3 years or less before death) to 40% reduction (6-7 years before death).

notes.description: Free-text notes about this lifetime transfer.
examples: Gift of family home to daughter — donor continued to live in the property, so gift with reservation rules apply
PET made 5 years before death — taper relief reduces IHT by 40%
